## [Note]

dbbot v0.11.0 tightens classic MHA support boundaries and improves release packaging hygiene.

Classic MHA is now explicitly scoped to MySQL 5.7 on CentOS 7 and Red Hat 7. New MySQL 8.4 and 9.7 primary-replica HA deployments should use MHA-Go instead.

## [Add]

- Show per-architecture version summaries in `dbbotctl support show`.
- Document that the official MySQL end-to-end test lab uses the `192.168.161.*` network while playbook default variables intentionally keep RFC 5737 `192.0.2.*` example addresses.

## [Change]

- Restrict the `mysql_mha` support matrix entry to CentOS 7 and Red Hat 7.
- Restrict `mysql_mha` package/version filtering to MySQL 5.7 package rules.
- Keep local release package artifacts outside the repository tree by default.
- Update classic MHA public documentation and support matrix pages to match the narrower support scope.

## [Fix]

- Fail `mha.yml` early when classic MHA is requested with a non-MySQL-5.7 version.
- Fail `mha.yml` early when classic MHA is requested on an operating system other than CentOS 7 or Red Hat 7.
